avoid: Do not promote this candidate; it is correct but not faster than baseline.
Do not write a custom full GEMM for W @ X.
Do not hardcode hidden dimensions beyond using the runtime d and fixed rank 16.
Do not materialize an explicit B.T transpose-copy unless profiling proves it is beneficial; SGEMM opB=T should handle this.
Do not use fragile handwritten rank-16 kernels before validating the pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM path.
Avoid U as column-major [16,d], m=16/n=d for SGEMM2, or leading dimension 16; those are common causes of incorrect pure cuBLAS candidates.
Do not use result caching as an optimization unless the benchmark explicitly uses repeated identical inputs as the target workload.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested dimensions 3584, 4096, and 4608 with max_abs_err=0 and rel_l2_err=0.
Aggregate speedup is essentially 1.0: 0.9999x versus the Torch baseline.
Per-shape latency matches Torch almost exactly: 3.725 ms vs 3.724 ms at d=3584, 5.820 ms vs 5.821 ms at d=4096, and 7.453 ms vs 7.452 ms at d=4608.
Candidate family is aten_reference, so it is not reducing ATen overhead or changing the computation strategy.
Profile labels the bottleneck as multiple ATen GEMM and add launches.
recommended_actions: Reject this candidate as an optimization because it is only the ATen baseline and provides no measurable speedup.
Move next to a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation instead of ATen matmul/add.
Keep W @ X as a direct cuBLAS SGEMM; do not attempt a custom full d x d GEMM because the main GEMM dominates and cuBLAS is the right implementation.
Use the qyh-style three-SGEMM mapping: SGEMM1 computes Y = W @ X using row-major-compatible cuBLAS arguments with opA=N/opB=N, A=X, B=W.
Compute the low-rank intermediate without explicitly materializing B.T: SGEMM2 should use opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, producing U with shape {d,16} and ldc=d.
Accumulate the low-rank output directly into Y: SGEMM3 should use opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, and beta=1 so that A @ (B.T @ X) is added into the existing Y.
Validate the pure cuBLAS path across all runtime d values 3584, 4096, and 4608 before considering lower-level custom rank-16 kernels.

If future pure cuBLAS attempts fail correctness, first inspect row-major/column-major interpretation, cuBLAS op flags, m/n/k values, leading dimensions, alpha, beta, and especially the U layout contract.
